result:
By default, React re-renders every child when the parent re-renders — props don't need to change. Common fixes:

1. **`React.memo`** — wrap the child; it skips re-renders when props are shallow-equal.
   ```jsx
   export default React.memo(Child)
   ```

2. **Stabilize props** — `memo` only helps if props are referentially stable. Inline objects/arrays/functions break it:
   ```jsx
   <Child onClick={() => doX()} config={{ a: 1 }} />  // new refs every render
   ```
   Wrap with `useCallback` / `useMemo` in the parent.

3. **Move state down** — if only part of the tree needs the state, colocate it there so the parent doesn't re-render.

4. **Split context** — a context consumer re-renders on every provider value change; split or memoize the value.

Use React DevTools' Profiler ("Why did this render?") to see which prop changed.

1. **`React.memo`** — wrap the child; it skips re-renders when props are shallow-equal.

2. **Stabilize props** — `memo` only helps if props are referentially stable. Inline objects/arrays/functions break it:

   Wrap with `useCallback` / `useMemo` in the parent.

3. **Move state down** — if only part of the tree needs the state, colocate it there so the parent doesn't re-render.

4. **Split context** — a context consumer re-renders on every provider value change; split or memoize the value.

Use React DevTools' Profiler ("Why did this render?") to see which prop changed.
